evals.prompt: Generate a VC investor memo for a Series B raise ($50-100M) for a 50MW data center campus in Phoenix, Arizona. The company controls 200 acres with 500MW of utility capacity secured, has LOIs for 30MW from two hyperscale tenants, and is led by a founding team with prior exits in infrastructure technology. The first 10MW phase is under construction. Competitive moat is site control (200 acres) and power access (500MW secured with 2-year head start on competitors). TAM is the $300B+ US data center market. Target exit via trade sale to REIT or infrastructure fund within 5-7 years.
evals.expected_output: A VC-structured memo leading with TAM ($300B+ market), growth trajectory (500MW buildable from 50MW start), and competitive moat (200-acre site control, 500MW utility capacity). Executive summary emphasizes first-mover advantage and hyperscale LOIs. Use of proceeds: Series B funds Phase 1 completion and Phase 2 pre-development, transitioning to project finance for subsequent phases. Team section highlights prior exits and domain expertise. Financial highlights: $/MW development cost advantage, customer pipeline, months to first revenue. Path to exit: trade sale to DC REIT at stabilized cap rates, with secondary sale and IPO as alternatives. Capital intensity paradox addressed: VC capital for platform and site control, project finance for construction.
assertions: Memo structure follows VC format with TAM, competitive moat, and exit path
Growth metrics (revenue CAGR, MW pipeline) are emphasized over yield metrics
Capital intensity paradox is addressed (why VC not project finance)
Team credentials and prior exits are prominently featured
Path to exit includes trade sale to REIT with timeline
Financial disclaimer includes 'not investment advice' language

evals.prompt: Create a PE/infrastructure investor memo for a stabilized 200MW hyperscale data center with 95% occupancy, contracted revenue from three hyperscale tenants on 10-15 year take-or-pay leases, and a DSCR of 1.45x. Total insured value $2B. Current NOI of $180M annually with 3% annual escalators. The facility has 100MW of expansion capacity on adjacent land. Capital structure: 60% senior debt at 5.5%, 40% equity. Reference FEDERAL-TAX-GUIDE.md for ITC on $50M qualifying solar CapEx and MACRS depreciation benefits. Target levered equity IRR of 12-15%.
evals.expected_output: A PE/infrastructure-structured memo leading with asset quality and stabilized yield. Asset overview: 200MW, 95% occupied, contracted revenue with 10-15 year tenors and 3% escalators. Stabilized NOI of $180M, cap rate context against comparable DC transactions. DSCR of 1.45x demonstrating strong debt service coverage. Downside protection: take-or-pay leases, creditworthy hyperscale tenants, long-term PPAs. Value creation: 100MW expansion potential on adjacent land. Tax benefits from FEDERAL-TAX-GUIDE.md: 30% ITC on $50M solar ($15M credit), MACRS depreciation enhancing levered returns. Key metrics: stabilized NOI, cap rate, levered IRR 12-15%, MOIC, debt yield.
assertions: Memo structure follows PE/infra format with yield, NOI, and cap rate emphasis
DSCR of 1.45x is prominently featured as debt service indicator
Contracted take-or-pay revenue provides downside protection narrative
FEDERAL-TAX-GUIDE.md is referenced for ITC and MACRS tax benefits
Expansion potential (100MW) is positioned as value creation opportunity
Financial disclaimer with 'not investment advice' and sensitivity caveat

evals.prompt: Draft a DFI investor memo for a 30MW sovereign AI facility in Nairobi, Kenya targeting IFC co-investment alongside private capital. The project creates 150 permanent jobs and 1,500 construction jobs, includes a technology transfer program with University of Nairobi and JKUAT, provides sovereign data hosting for Kenyan government agencies, and enables digital economy growth aligned with Kenya's Digital Economy Blueprint. Total investment $400M with DFI providing $100M concessional debt. Reference DFI-FUNDING.md for IFC Performance Standards alignment and AfDB strategic priorities.
evals.expected_output: A DFI-structured memo leading with DEVELOPMENT IMPACT before financial returns. Impact thesis: 150 permanent jobs, 1,500 construction jobs, technology transfer to Kenyan universities, sovereign data hosting enabling government digital services, digital economy growth for 50M+ Kenyans. ESG alignment: Scope 1/2 emissions commitment, water efficiency, community benefit fund. DFI-FUNDING.md referenced for IFC Performance Standards (PS1-PS8 compliance), AfDB Strategy 2024-2033 alignment (digital infrastructure pillar). Concessional structure: $100M DFI debt de-risks $300M private investment, blended finance approach. Kenya Digital Economy Blueprint alignment explicitly mapped. Financial summary comes AFTER impact thesis.
assertions: Development impact thesis appears BEFORE financial returns in memo structure
Job creation (permanent and construction) is quantified prominently
DFI-FUNDING.md is referenced for IFC Performance Standards alignment
Technology transfer program with specific universities is featured
Concessional financing structure explains DFI capital de-risking private investment
Kenya Digital Economy Blueprint is referenced by name
